goal: Give Erik a 60-second green/yellow/red verdict on Lena's demo_cell_v4 scene — is it customer-ready or not.
thoughts: Erik has 5 minutes. Start with scene_summary, not a Python shell. Customer-visible issues (missing textures, default grey, z-fighting, unlit corners) matter more than engineering edge cases. Functional risks = G1 joint limits, disabled colliders, kinematicEnabled=True on things that should be dynamic. Do NOT dump 400 prim paths. Commit to a verdict, do not hedge.
code:
# 1. Bird's-eye view, no Python shell
scene_summary(stage_path="omniverse://localhost/Projects/Picking/demo_cell_v4.usd")

# 2. One-shot quality sweep — materials, colliders, unapplied schemas
run_stage_analysis(scope="/World")

# 3. Ground the customer-visible checklist
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim demo readiness checklist textures z-fighting lighting MDL")

# Output template for Slack to Lena (exactly 3 bullets):
# GREEN:  <e.g. scripted cycle, physics stable, all colliders active>
# YELLOW: <e.g. 3 boxes still on default grey MDL, customer will notice>
# RED:    <e.g. conveyor belt mesh has no assigned material, or kinematicEnabled=True on Robot base>
# Verdict: <go | needs-fix-by-Thursday | pull-demo>
failure_modes: Dumping 400 lines of list_all_prims output — Erik bails
Hedging (no verdict) — Erik needs a call to paste to Lena
Missing the VP-of-logistics POV (texture/motion/collisions, not code)
Engineering-only lens that ignores cosmetic customer-visible issues
